sourceMovie.title: Cinderella
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Cinderella (2015) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Beauty and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cinderella clicked for you, Beauty and the Beast (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cinderella clicked for you, Maleficent (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cinderella?
faq.answer: Try Beauty and the Beast, Maleficent, Enchanted—each shares DNA with Cinderella while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cinderella?
faq.answer: Cinderella (2015) is best described as Romance, Fantasy, Family, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cinderella worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
